summary: The axilla is a pyramid-shaped space with its apex directed towards the root of the neck.
The axilla is a cylindrical space with its apex directed towards the thorax.
The axilla is a cube-shaped space located entirely within the thoracic cage.
The brachial plexus arises from ventral rami of C5-T1, and injury to the upper trunk (C5-C6) causes Erb's palsy.
The brachial plexus arises from C1-C5, and injury to the upper trunk causes Klumpke's palsy.
The brachial plexus arises from T1-T5, and injury causes wrist drop.
Klumpke's paralysis results from lower trunk (C8-T1) injury and presents with claw hand.
Klumpke's paralysis results from upper trunk injury and presents with waiter's tip position.
Klumpke's paralysis results from posterior cord injury and causes wrist drop.
The rotator cuff muscles are supraspinatus, infraspinatus, teres minor, and subscapularis (SITS).
The rotator cuff muscles are deltoid, supraspinatus, infraspinatus, and teres major.
The rotator cuff muscles are trapezius, rhomboids, levator scapulae, and serratus anterior.
Supraspinatus is the most commonly torn rotator cuff muscle, especially in abduction injuries.
Infraspinatus is the most commonly torn rotator cuff muscle.
Subscapularis is the most commonly torn rotator cuff muscle.
Paralysis of serratus anterior (long thoracic nerve injury) causes winging of the scapula.
Paralysis of trapezius causes winging of the scapula.
Paralysis of rhomboid major causes winging of the scapula.
The axillary nerve wraps around the surgical neck of the humerus and can be injured in shoulder dislocations.
The axillary nerve wraps around the anatomical neck of the humerus.
The radial nerve wraps around the surgical neck of the humerus.
The axillary artery is divided into three parts by pectoralis minor, with 1, 2, and 3 branches respectively.
The axillary artery is divided into three parts by pectoralis major.
The axillary artery is divided into two parts by the coracobrachialis.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the axilla and shoulder anatomy and try again.
